codeReview.summary: The feature adds the intended config layers and coverage, but two blocking correctness gaps remain in the shipped implementation.

issues.description: `loadDirectoryScopedReviews` returns the first deepest `REVIEW.md` found across all changed files, so changes spanning multiple directories discard sibling directory-scoped instructions instead of applying the nearest `REVIEW.md` per matching file as required. In a run touching `pkg/util.go` and `src/auth/login.go`, only one directory review is loaded globally, so one file is reviewed under the wrong instructions.

The loader explicitly discards all parse/validation warnings from `ParseAIReviewConfig` (`cfg, _, _ := ParseAIReviewConfig(aiBody)`), and the parser itself accepts unknown YAML fields silently. That means `.gitlab/ai-review.yaml` is not actually validating known fields as the feature contract requires: misspelled or unsupported keys are ignored with no surfaced warning, so invalid repository config can appear to apply successfully.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The beta config-layer worker established that current rule loading stores a single `TrustedRules.ReviewMarkdown` string, but beta requirements now need directory-scoped `REVIEW.md` to apply per changed file, not as one global winner. Shared-state docs do not capture this architectural limitation or the need for per-path trusted instruction scoping.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: internal/context/assembler.go:98-102 defines one `TrustedRules.ReviewMarkdown` field; internal/rules/loader.go:139-144 replaces root review text with a single directory review for the whole run.

summary: Reviewed handoff, transcript skeleton, skill, shared-state docs, and commit `c33ab81f7a101eebf13ea2528f4a268d4218fdf8`. The implementation is close, but it fails the per-directory REVIEW.md semantics and does not truly validate/surface invalid `.gitlab/ai-review.yaml` fields, so the feature should not pass scrutiny yet.
